tools.name: githubDotComWebhook.cloudbuildGithubDotComWebhookReceive
tools.description: ReceiveGitHubDotComWebhook is called when the API receives a github.com webhook.

tools.name: locations.cloudbuildLocationsRegionalWebhook
tools.description: ReceiveRegionalWebhook is called when the API receives a regional GitHub webhook.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsBuildsCancel
tools.description: Cancels a build in progress.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsBuildsCreate
tools.description: Starts a build with the specified configuration. This method returns a long-running `Operation`, which includes the build ID. Pass the build ID to `GetBuild` to determine the build status (such as `SUCCESS` or `FAILURE`).

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsBuildsGet
tools.description: Returns information about a previously requested build. The `Build` that is returned includes its status (such as `SUCCESS`, `FAILURE`, or `WORKING`), and timing information.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsBuildsList
tools.description: Lists previously requested builds. Previously requested builds may still be in-progress, or may have finished successfully or unsuccessfully.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsBuildsRetry
tools.description: Creates a new build based on the specified build. This method creates a new build using the original build request, which may or may not result in an identical build. For triggered builds: * Triggered builds resolve to a precise revision; therefore a retry of a triggered build will result in a build that uses the same revision. For non-triggered builds that specify `RepoSource`: * If the original build built from the tip of a branch, the retried build will build from the tip of that branch, which may not be the same revision as the original build. * If the original build specified a commit sha or revision ID, the retried build will use the identical source. For builds that specify `StorageSource`: * If the original build pulled source from Google Cloud Storage without specifying the generation of the object, the new build will use the current object, which may be different from the original build source. * If the original build pulled source from Cloud Storage and specified the generation of the object, the new build will attempt to use the same object, which may or may not be available depending on the bucket's lifecycle management settings.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsBitbucketServerConfigsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new `BitbucketServerConfig`. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsBitbucketServerConfigsList
tools.description: List all `BitbucketServerConfigs` for a given project. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsBitbucketServerConfigsRemoveBitbucketServerConnectedRepository
tools.description: Remove a Bitbucket Server repository from a given BitbucketServerConfig's connected repositories. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsBuildsApprove
tools.description: Approves or rejects a pending build. If approved, the returned LRO will be analogous to the LRO returned from a CreateBuild call. If rejected, the returned LRO will be immediately done.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsBuildsCreate
tools.description: Starts a build with the specified configuration. This method returns a long-running `Operation`, which includes the build ID. Pass the build ID to `GetBuild` to determine the build status (such as `SUCCESS` or `FAILURE`).

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsBuildsList
tools.description: Lists previously requested builds. Previously requested builds may still be in-progress, or may have finished successfully or unsuccessfully.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsBuildsRetry
tools.description: Creates a new build based on the specified build. This method creates a new build using the original build request, which may or may not result in an identical build. For triggered builds: * Triggered builds resolve to a precise revision; therefore a retry of a triggered build will result in a build that uses the same revision. For non-triggered builds that specify `RepoSource`: * If the original build built from the tip of a branch, the retried build will build from the tip of that branch, which may not be the same revision as the original build. * If the original build specified a commit sha or revision ID, the retried build will use the identical source. For builds that specify `StorageSource`: * If the original build pulled source from Google Cloud Storage without specifying the generation of the object, the new build will use the current object, which may be different from the original build source. * If the original build pulled source from Cloud Storage and specified the generation of the object, the new build will attempt to use the same object, which may or may not be available depending on the bucket's lifecycle management settings.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsGithubEnterpriseConfigsCreate
tools.description: Create an association between a GCP project and a GitHub Enterprise server.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsGithubEnterpriseConfigsList
tools.description: List all GitHubEnterpriseConfigs for a given project.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsGitLabConfigsConnectedRepositoriesBatchCreate
tools.description: Batch connecting GitLab repositories to Cloud Build. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsGitLabConfigsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new `GitLabConfig`. This API is experimental

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsGitLabConfigsList
tools.description: List all `GitLabConfigs` for a given project. This API is experimental

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsGitLabConfigsRemoveGitLabConnectedRepository
tools.description: Remove a GitLab repository from a given GitLabConfig's connected repositories. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsGitLabConfigsReposList
tools.description: List all repositories for a given `GitLabConfig`. This API is experimental

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsOperationsCancel
tools.description: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of 1, corresponding to `Code.CANCELLED`.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsTriggersCreate
tools.description: Creates a new `BuildTrigger`. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsTriggersList
tools.description: Lists existing `BuildTrigger`s. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsTriggersPatch
tools.description: Updates a `BuildTrigger` by its project ID and trigger ID. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsTriggersRun
tools.description: Runs a `BuildTrigger` at a particular source revision. To run a regional or global trigger, use the POST request that includes the location endpoint in the path (ex. v1/projects/{projectId}/locations/{region}/triggers/{triggerId}:run). The POST request that does not include the location endpoint in the path can only be used when running global triggers.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsTriggersWebhook
tools.description: ReceiveTriggerWebhook [Experimental] is called when the API receives a webhook request targeted at a specific trigger.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsWorkerPoolsCreate
tools.description: Creates a `WorkerPool`.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsWorkerPoolsDelete
tools.description: Deletes a `WorkerPool`.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsWorkerPoolsGet
tools.description: Returns details of a `WorkerPool`.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsWorkerPoolsList
tools.description: Lists `WorkerPool`s.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsLocationsWorkerPoolsPatch
tools.description: Updates a `WorkerPool`.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsTriggersCreate
tools.description: Creates a new `BuildTrigger`. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsTriggersDelete

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsTriggersGet
tools.description: Returns information about a `BuildTrigger`. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsTriggersList
tools.description: Lists existing `BuildTrigger`s. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsTriggersPatch
tools.description: Updates a `BuildTrigger` by its project ID and trigger ID. This API is experimental.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsTriggersRun
tools.description: Runs a `BuildTrigger` at a particular source revision. To run a regional or global trigger, use the POST request that includes the location endpoint in the path (ex. v1/projects/{projectId}/locations/{region}/triggers/{triggerId}:run). The POST request that does not include the location endpoint in the path can only be used when running global triggers.

tools.name: projects.cloudbuildProjectsTriggersWebhook
tools.description: ReceiveTriggerWebhook [Experimental] is called when the API receives a webhook request targeted at a specific trigger.
